title: Lithocarpus mairei
intro: Lithocarpus mairei, also known as the Maire's Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus mairei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lithocarpus mairei is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in the form of capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus mairei is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Fagales, the family Fagaceae, the genus Lithocarpus, and the species L. mairei. This classification indicates its position within the plant kingdom and its relationships to other species. The family Fagaceae, to which Lithocarpus mairei belongs, is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees commonly known as beech and oak trees. The genus Lithocarpus is a group of trees in the family Fagaceae that are native to Asia and are commonly known as stone oaks. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships between different species of plants.
